frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: machine-learning decoding of fMRI activity to predict experiential vs physiological measures

frameworks.name: biosignal proxy approach
frameworks.one_line: view that physiological responses can substitute for subjective experience in affective disorders

frameworks.name: metacognition / conscious perception
frameworks.one_line: framework linking prefrontal regions to subjective awareness

core_question: Do whole-brain multivoxel patterns predicting subjective fear coincide with those predicting physiological reactivity?
key_assumptions: subjective fear and skin conductance can be independently decoded from fMRI
decoder overlap reflects shared neural substrate
pictures of feared animals reliably elicit fear-related responses
manipulations: presentation of feared-animal images during fMRI
training separate decoders for subjective fear ratings vs skin conductance reactivity
key_findings: subjective fear and physiological responses were correlated in general
whole-brain decoders for the two measures were not identical
amygdala and insula primarily predicted physiological reactivity
prefrontal regions tied to metacognition primarily predicted subjective fear
interpretations: subjective experience and objective physiology have dissociable neural representations
the biosignal proxy approach for affective pathology is called into question
